intro: Sericanthe auriculata is a medicinal plant species, belonging to the Rubiaceae family, native to tropical Africa. The health benefits of Sericanthe auriculata include its traditional use in treating fever, inflammation, and as an antimalarial agent, with research suggesting its potential in inhibiting the growth of certain pathogens. The bioactive constituents of Sericanthe auriculata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions, and its bioactive constituents are also isolated and studied for potential pharmaceutical applications.
